prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (down, up, up) with high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.558) and high seasonality variance (0.5589). FT_catboost leads in trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf/DWT_svr), indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. Given the significant model disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "DWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "DWT_svr", "FT_svr", "ARIMA"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.558,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show mixed trend directions (down, up, up) with high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.558) and high seasonality variance (0.5589). FT_catboost leads in trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf/DWT_svr), indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. Given the significant model disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "dba_combination"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.558 (high dispersion) favors rmse_focus preset and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 ≤ 4 justifies small top_k=3. DBA combination recommended by pattern_analyst for high seasonality_variance=0.5589. Debate forced as top candidates are competitive."
}

All types represented. No leakage detected - n_windows=3 with expanding rolling mode. Score margin top2 is small (0.0208) indicating competitive set. dba_combination wins most horizons (6/12) but best_per_horizon_by_validation has highest score (1.000). Set is balanced and diverse - no changes needed.",

FT_catboost leads in trend tracking while ONLY_CWT_svr excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf/DWT_svr), indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. Given the significant model disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors.

RMSE spread_ratio=0.558 (high dispersion) favors rmse_focus preset and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 ≤ 4 justifies small top_k=3. DBA combination recommended by pattern_analyst for high seasonality_variance=0.5589. Debate forced as top candidates are competitive.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All types represented. No leakage detected - n_windows=3 with expanding rolling mode.
